page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-176 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C processors. It features 176 virtual CPUs across 88 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1408.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 146417.7 and a Passmark CPU score of 104670.37. The server exhibits high memory bandwidth, reaching 11970.86 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 446.56 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While single-core performance remains in the average tier with a Geekbench single-core score of 1723.0, multi-threaded workloads show strong execution, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 18209.0 and top-tier performance in ray tracing and image processing.

Given the high resource density of 176 vCPUs and 1408 GB of RAM, this server offers a specialized hardware profile designed for memory-intensive and highly parallelized workloads. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions enhances its capability for vector and matrix operations without requiring discrete GPU hardware. This makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and heavy multi-threaded compilation or data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-176 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 176 dedicated vCPUs and 1408.0 GB of RAM, powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 2.7 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 146417.7 and a Passmark CPU score of 104670.37. It also delivers exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 446.56 GB/sec for uncached large block reads, and outstanding database performance with 164.09 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance is average, the massive memory capacity and advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Intel AMX and AVX-512, make this server highly efficient for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and parallelized data processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-176 is an x86_64 server with 176 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1408 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-176 features 176 vCPUs and 1408 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 176 dedicated vCPUs, designed for massive in-memory databases and parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 176 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz.
1408 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth up to 11970.86 GB/sec.
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions.
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included.
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and parallel data processing.
